meta.primaryUse: App-based contest platform for baristas with ranking, discovery, and reputation
meta.productVision: Build a mobile-first platform where baristas can enter contests, gain verified exposure, build portable reputation, and get discovered by cafes, brands, and event organizers.
meta.coreThesis: This is not just a contest app. It is a reputation engine, media engine, and talent marketplace built around coffee skill performance.

executiveSummary.problem: Baristas have limited pathways for visibility outside local cafe scenes, traditional associations, or one-off live throwdowns. Existing systems are fragmented, gatekept, and often reward proximity, popularity, or institutional access more than skill.
executiveSummary.solution: Create a contest-native app where baristas submit performance content, compete in structured challenges, receive transparent scores, build ranked profiles, and translate performance into opportunity.
executiveSummary.businessPremise: Start with barista contests and creator-style engagement. Expand into sponsorships, hiring, event hosting, analytics, education, and eventually a parallel performance-based credential ecosystem.

goals.goal: Give baristas a repeatable way to gain visibility beyond local geography.
Create transparent, auditable contest scoring that reduces black-box judging.
Turn skill performance into a durable reputation profile.
Build viral content loops around contests, voting, and rankings.
Create a discovery surface for cafes, sponsors, and event organizers.
Establish the foundation for future standards and certification alternatives.

nonGoals.nonGoal: Not a general social network for all coffee content at launch.
Not a full e-commerce marketplace in V1.
Not a formal accreditation body in V1.
Not a full POS, cafe operations, or training LMS product initially.

segment.name: Competitive Baristas
segment.description: Baristas who want to showcase skill, gain recognition, compete remotely, and grow a public profile.

segment.name: Cafe Owners and Managers
segment.description: Businesses looking to discover talent, host contests, and build local brand engagement.

segment.name: Coffee Brands and Sponsors
segment.description: Roasters, milk brands, equipment brands, and coffee-adjacent sponsors seeking authentic reach.

segment.name: Judges and Educators
segment.description: Credible professionals who want to judge, mentor, build influence, and be associated with quality.

segment.name: Coffee Enthusiasts / Audience
segment.description: Fans who want to watch, vote, follow baristas, and engage in coffee culture.

pillar.name: Merit Visibility
pillar.definition: Skill performance should create exposure, not gatekeeping or insider access.

pillar.name: Transparent Competition
pillar.definition: Rules, judging weights, and outcomes should be visible and explainable.

pillar.name: Portable Reputation
pillar.definition: A barista's profile should act as a living resume tied to verified performance.

pillar.name: Culture as Distribution
pillar.definition: Contests should also function as content and community growth loops.

pillar.name: Opportunity Conversion
pillar.definition: Recognition should translate into gigs, hires, sponsorships, and real-world advancement.

included.item: One primary contest type: global open submission latte art challenge
